mnemonics: Swing is L.A.F. - Look And Feel. Remember that Swing components are lightweight and their appearance (Look) can be changed (Feel) with pluggable look-and-feels.
keyPoints: Swing is a set of GUI components that are lightweight and platform-independent.
It is built on top of the AWT (Abstract Window Toolkit) and provides a more extensive set of components than AWT.
Swing components follow the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture, which separates the data, presentation, and control logic.
commonMistakes: Mixing heavy-weight AWT components (e.g., Button) with light-weight Swing components (e.g., JButton), which can lead to painting issues and z-order problems.
Not performing GUI updates on the Event Dispatch Thread (EDT), which can cause unpredictable behavior and thread-safety issues.
